g.text: Page Binary Layout — Slotted Page (4096 bytes)

ul.li: Header + pointers: fixed position, grow toward middle
Cell data: packed from page end, grow toward middle
Page full when pointer array meets cell content area
Pointers are sorted by key; cells are insertion-ordered
Indirection via pointers enables compaction without external invalidation

div.p: Byte Order: Big-Endian All multi-byte integers (uint16, uint32) stored big-endian for cross-platform portability

g.text: Cell Pointer Array — grows downward from offset 0x0C

div.p: FREE SPACE (unallocated region between pointer array and cell content)

g.text: Cell Content Area — grows upward from offset 0xFFF
0x01 | uint16_t | first_freeblock // BE, offset to first free block (0=none)
0x03 | uint16_t | cell_count // BE, number of cells on this page
0x05 | uint16_t | cell_content_start // BE, offset to start of cell content
0x07 | uint8_t | fragmented_bytes // total fragmented free bytes
0x08 | uint32_t | right_child // BE, right-most child page (internal only)
0x0C | uint16_t | cell_ptr[0] → offset of cell 0 in content area
0x0E | uint16_t | cell_ptr[1] → offset of cell 1 in content area
0x10 | uint16_t | cell_ptr[2] → offset of cell 2 in content area
... | uint16_t | cell_ptr[N-1] → offset of cell N-1
Pointers sorted by key order; cells in content area are NOT sorted
Cell Content (variable-length cells, packed from bottom)
near 0xFFF | ... | Cell 0 (first inserted, near page bottom)
Cells written in insertion order (not key order)
varint | payload_size // total bytes of record data
4 bytes BE | left_child_page // page number of left subtree
No payload — internal nodes store only navigation keys
